---
title: "ab_metadata"
author: "Sam Bogan"
date: "2026-04-29"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# Load libraries
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)

```

Wrangle NCBI metadata into tabular df and csv

```{r}

# Read the raw text (replace with your file path if needed)
txt <- readLines(
  "~/Documents/GitHub/repadapt_poecilid_vcfs/metadata/abalone/biosample_result.txt"
  )

# Collapse into one string
txt <- paste(txt, collapse = "\n")

# Split entries by leading number
entries <- str_split(txt, "\n\\d+:\\s+")[[1]]

# Remove empty first element if present
entries <- entries[entries != ""]

# Function to parse one entry
parse_entry <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    sample_id = str_extract(x, "^[^\\n]+"),
    biosample = str_match(x, "BioSample:\\s*(\\S+)")[,2],
    sample_name = str_match(x, "Sample name:\\s*(\\S+)")[,2],
    sra = str_match(x, "SRA:\\s*(\\S+)")[,2],
    organism = str_match(x, "Organism:\\s*([^\\n]+)")[,2],
    collection_date = str_match(x, 'collection date="([^"]+)"')[,2],
    geographic_location = str_match(x, 'geographic location="([^"]+)"')[,2],
    tissue = str_match(x, 'tissue="([^"]+)"')[,2],
    collected_by = str_match(x, 'collected by="([^"]+)"')[,2],
    isolate = str_match(x, 'isolate="([^"]+)"')[,2],
    isolation_source = str_match(x, 'isolation source="([^"]+)"')[,2],
    lat_long = str_match(x, 'latitude and longitude="([^"]+)"')[,2],
    accession = str_match(x, "Accession:\\s*(\\S+)")[,2],
    id = str_match(x, "ID:\\s*(\\S+)")[,2]
  )
}

# Apply parser to all entries
df <- map_dfr(entries, parse_entry)

# Optional: split latitude/longitude into numeric columns
df <- df %>%
  separate(lat_long, into = c("lat", "lon"), sep = " N | W", remove = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    lat = as.numeric(lat),
    lon = -as.numeric(lon)  # western hemisphere negative
  )

# Remove semicolons
df$biosample <- gsub(";", "", df$biosample)
df$sample_name <- gsub(";", "", df$sample_name)

# Write to CSV
write.csv(df, "biosample_metadata_tabular.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Preview
print(df)

# What 

```
